column12: Measurement condition : Ice; Clear , No Precipitation , Calm Wind
column14: Normal mmnt section had much wider profile than normal. Bridged ice flow was on the rhb this time much like the open lead we found last year in nov visit. Water quality samples not taken as we did not expect to get here for a visit today. Only 1 dwl for mgh as that's just how it goes here based on the distance between gauge and mint section. HFC Comments: Control Condition: CICMeasurement Section Codition: CICWeather Condition: PARLY CLOUDY ice edge surveyed as water level transfer approx. .285m above current wl.. tank is partial empty from stewart mouth as the full one we brought was mt. did not reset gauge as sporatic trace with gas depleted. Wait to see where it recovers to before putting in corrections. Line purged and open. Fixed small leak on out line. Mid-section

column12: Measurement condition : Clear; Partly Cloudy , No Precipitation , Calm Wind
column13: Open. Possible change based on assumption due to high water and big ice event during break up.
column14: Flow tracker easy wader. Mmnt plot well to the left. Some trees down below gauge at cw out about 2 m right at cw mmnt xsect.we discussed it and not likely ctreating 13%shift.  @ Uncertainty: IVE method, 2-sigma value (2 x Uncertainty Value reported in *.ft File). @. Ft2mmnt import says dates dont match. Time of mmnt on when says -8hrs. Something fishy. I think it has to do with the utc time thinkinking the mmnt is too. -8 correction in ft2 shows up. Mean time works out. Print out shows 21rst so I changed start and end time manually. @ Uncertainty: IVE method, 2-sigma value (2 x Uncertainty Value reported in *.ft File). @ @ Uncertainty: IVE method, 2-sigma value (2 x Uncertainty Value reported in *.ft File). @ Logger data not collected. He thought I did and I thought he did. Bm tie done. Cut collected twice. Sensor purged and took some time to stabilize.  Waited till after mmnt to reset hg. Mid-section

column12: Measurement condition : Clear; Partly Cloudy , No Precipitation , Calm Wind
column14: @ Uncertainty: QRev Uncertainty Analysis (DS Mueller, 2016), 2-sigma value (1 x Uncertainty Value reported in *.xml File). @ no shifting required. excellent calm mmnt with fly fox. Painty trace at reset. Old .756 new .628 ADCP by Moving Boat

column12: Measurement condition : Ice; Cloudy , Heavy Snow ,
column14: CIC. Two channels and slush/sand island in middle, just like last mmnt. @ Uncertainty: IVE method, 2-sigma value (2 x Uncertainty Value reported in *.ft File). @ Line is frozen - no valid logger readings. Using DWL as MGH. One 900lb bottle on site, one full. Inserted methyl to see if line thaws out. Obtained water quality/dissolved metals sample. Mid-section

column12: Measurement condition : Ice; Partly Cloudy ,  ,
column13: CIC, ice cover slumped on LB and RB, rise @ mid-channel.
column14: Good x-section @ helipad. Flow in two separate channels on LB and RB with slush to bed @ mid-channel. One pannel >10%, need to get out to get 60 mile before end of daylight. Left as-is. Ref BM6 - not control but no movement. No GC required. Purged line after changing empty tank, sounds healthy but unsure of validity of logger data up until tank change. To establish once have a better look at trace. Using DWL as MGH for this reason. Reset logger but line may have been equalizing. May have to apply offset after SRC to where equalization occurs on trace. Installed 900lb tank, found previous empty but holding pressure. Approx 60cm snow on ground. Ice cover frozen to bed mid-channel. Mid-section
GC: 0.000 HG: 0.780 Reset (RS)

column12: Measurement condition : Ice; Clear , No Precipitation , Calm Wind
column13: mostly CIC but unusual open leads present d/s of helipad
column14: ***After talking with CM, confirmed flow on LB was not measured, making this measurement invalid. Measurement on Jan 26th found just under half the flow on RB, and over half on LB. Doubled flow measured on Nov 30th to guide the recession (based on flow location in January). -EJB 2023/02/27. Mmnt taken d/s of helipad just above open lead. tried to meaure at helipad but section was very wide and an unusual cross section for this trip. deffinately not all the flow on the LHB like normal. good flast flowing mmnt. 2 panels had bridged ice. did not correct HFC for different coefficient or to .6 shot. considered minimal impact of bridged ice condition next two other panels that had minimal ice affected velocites.  HFC Comments: Control Condition: cicMeasurement Section Codition: bWeather Condition: 100ccWind Velocity: light

column12: Measurement condition : Clear; Cloudy , Mist , Calm Wind
column14: Disregarded first 2 transects due to SPro temperature acclimatization.  Suspect channel deposition downstream after July high water. Flying Fox rope wrapped around cable. Cannot use. Waded across with SPro + Trimaran attached 10m D/S. Not sure why this measurement plots so far off last measurements. Could there have been significant deposition after the high water event of July (mmnt plotted +11.7%)?  Should have obtained a second measurement but it was a long visit of chain-sawing many trees around helipad, along the trail, in front of BMs, trying to fix Flying Fox rope, followed by the same at Stewart R nr the Mouth. @ Uncertainty: QRev Uncertainty Analysis (DS Mueller, 2016), 2-sigma value (1 x Uncertainty Value reported in *.xml File). @ Laminar flow. SRC as expected from previous OM noticed on trace. Cleared trail with chainsaw, cleared helipad with brush saw. ***Need to fix Flying Fox rope (FIXED JAN 2023). ***Need to cut tree just below cableway in winter - overhanging dangerously. Should be cut from top of ice (CUT DOWN JAN 2023). ADCP by Moving Boat

column12: Measurement condition : Clear; Cloudy , Light Rain , Calm Wind
column13: RB is undercut. Lower bench appears changed.
column14: Good ARC Boat mmnt @ landing pad. Flow is in grasses. Signs of shoreline eroding and undercutting after this last very high water event, matches shift well. To confirm in September with updated GC. .  Uncertainty: QRev Uncertainty Analysis (DS Mueller, 2016), 2-sigma value (1 x Uncertainty Value reported in *.xml File). @ GC of -0.034 not applied in logger - ONLY ON TRACE AND MGH. Obtained good HWM @ 3.399m. Discernable OM on trace confirmed by level survey, opposite of suspected movement. Purged line to clear and verify that line is still submerged. Water level came down to very close to DWL after purge. Line is approx 2m away from shore. Good bubbles. 2 spare armoured conduit lines in gauge. ADCP by Moving Boat

column13: Ice on RB/home shore does not seem to affect flow/is pushed up on shore.
column14: A lot of cavitation and air entrainment of props/transducer in turbulent water. Loss of BT throughout, especially in a particular spot close to RB, no reason observed. Using GGA seems to get rid of the BT loss issues and the lack of a good quality moving bed test. Ice on edges. Line is healthy. HWM = 3.490m. GC applied. ADCP by Moving Boat

column14: Channel is dry from RB to 2/3 of x-section. Flow is concentrated along LB, as per past years. HFC Comments: Only 13 pannels - drilled/measured at 0.25m increments. column14: All flow is concentrated from mid-channel to LB. Branches in holes to mark both edges of flow. Very shallow.  Ice edge surveyed at 1.911m (attached to shore). Shore ice collapsed DS at mmnt section on LB, approx. 0.6m height difference. Shore and channel ice intact at gauge. Mid-section

column14: Slightest amount of ice on shore. Doubt it would create any kind of backwater yet. Great FT2 mmnt with tree cleared out of X-Section. May MMNT plotted 17%; July MMNT plotted 12%; This MMNT plots 7%. Seems fitting for this station. Apply shift found. Chainsawed tree resting on cableway. Cleared. SRC found same as in July. Confirmed. Reset water level after resetting orifice block as deep as possible in water.   Mid-section
